turns: I've been feeling really uneasy lately. Not sure why.
Maybe it's because my son hasn't called in two weeks.
I don't want to bother him, he's busy.
Some days the house feels so empty.
I miss having someone to share meals with.
I just got back from a trip with my grandchildren!
It was wonderful. I feel so alive.

scenarios.persona_label: Person processing the loss of a spouse
turns: My husband passed away three months ago.
I keep finding his things around the house and it breaks me.
People say I should donate his clothes but I can't.
My daughter thinks I'm not processing it right.
What does processing grief even mean?
I cry every day. Is that normal?
Will it ever stop hurting this much?

scenarios.persona_label: Parent dealing with difficult adult child
turns: My son and I had a huge fight last week.
He says I'm too controlling but I'm just trying to help.
He's 45 and I still worry about him.
He hung up on me and hasn't called back.
Should I call him first? I don't want to seem desperate.
I raised him alone after his father left. That has to count for something.

scenarios.persona_label: Senior anxious about a medical diagnosis
turns: The doctor found something in my bloodwork.
She said not to worry but how can I not?
I have a follow-up appointment next week.
I haven't told my family yet.
What if it's serious?
I've been googling symptoms and now I'm terrified.
How do I stop spiraling?

scenarios.persona_label: Insomniac with racing thoughts at night
turns: I haven't slept well in weeks.
Every night I lie awake thinking about everything.
It's usually about things I can't control.
My doctor suggested melatonin but it's not helping.
I'm exhausted during the day but can't sleep at night.
My husband sleeps right through it and that makes me jealous and annoyed.

scenarios.persona_label: Elderly person experiencing prolonged low mood
turns: I don't feel like myself anymore.
I used to love gardening but I haven't touched the plants in months.
Nothing really excites me.
I eat, sleep, watch TV. That's about it.
My friends have stopped calling. I think I pushed them away.
I don't answer the phone when they do call.
I know that makes it worse but I don't have energy for conversations.
My doctor says I might be depressed but that feels like a label.
I'm 72. Isn't this just what getting old is?
My late wife would be so disappointed in me.
She was always the strong one.
Sometimes I wonder what the point is.
Not in a dangerous way, just... what am I still doing here.
I have grandkids who need me. That keeps me going.
How do I find meaning again?

scenarios.persona_label: Older adult in a troubled marriage
turns: My wife and I have been married 40 years.
Lately it feels like we're just roommates.
We don't fight. We just don't talk either.
I tried to have a conversation last night and she just looked at her phone.
I brought her flowers once and she said 'what did you do wrong?'.
That hurt more than I expected.
I wonder if we ever really knew each other.
Our kids are grown and gone. This emptiness is new.
I suggested counseling and she laughed.
Is it too late at our age to work on a marriage?
I don't want to be alone but I also don't want to feel invisible.
Sometimes I think about moving to a smaller place by myself.
That terrifies me too.
Me siento muy nerviosa últimamente.
Mis nietos viven lejos y no los veo seguido.
Rezo por ellos pero igual me preocupo.
Hoy cumplí 70 años y me siento muy bien.
Mis hijos me organizaron una fiesta sorpresa.
Me siento muy querida.
Tuve un problema fuerte con mi hija mayor.
Me dijo que me meto demasiado en su vida.
Solo quiero ayudar, soy su madre.
Ella dice que la asfixio.
No sé cómo darle espacio sin sentir que la pierdo.
¿Cómo puedo ser menos entrometida sin dejar de cuidarla?
No quiero perder la relación con ella.
Desde que murió mi esposa ya nada es igual.
Vivimos juntos 45 años.
Ahora la casa me parece enorme y vacía.
Mis amigos se preocupan pero no saben qué decirme.
Yo tampoco sé qué necesito.
Solo sé que me duele estar solo.

scenarios.persona_label: Adulto mayor con ansiedad por su salud
turns: El médico me mandó unos análisis adicionales.
No me dijo nada concreto, solo que espere los resultados.
Llevo cinco días esperando y me tiene muy angustiado.
Le pregunté a mi hija y me dijo que no me preocupe pero ¿cómo no hacerlo?
He estado pensando en lo peor.
¿Cómo se puede estar tranquilo en estas situaciones?
Trabajo muy duro pero siento que nadie lo aprecia.
Mi jefe siempre encuentra algo que criticar.
Llevo 20 años en esta empresa.
Me da coraje que los jóvenes nuevos suben más rápido que yo.
No sé si seguir ahí o buscar otra cosa a mis 58 años.
Me da miedo no encontrar nada mejor.

scenarios.persona_label: Persona procesando la muerte de un padre
turns: Mi papá murió hace dos meses.
Era mi mejor amigo además de mi padre.
No tuvimos oportunidad de despedirnos bien, fue muy rápido.
Tenía cosas pendientes que decirle.
Me arrepiento de no haberlas dicho cuando podía.
Mi mamá está muy mal también y yo tengo que ser fuerte para ella.
No sé dónde meter mis propios sentimientos.
Lloro cuando estoy solo en el coche.
En el trabajo finjo que estoy bien.
¿Es normal sentirse así después de dos meses?
A veces me enojo con él por haberse ido.
Sé que es irracional pero no puedo evitarlo.
¿Cómo ayudo a mi mamá mientras yo mismo estoy roto?

scenarios.persona_label: Persona con síntomas de depresión prolongada
turns: Me cuesta levantarme por las mañanas.
Antes era muy activo, ahora no tengo ganas de nada.
Dejé de ver a mis amigos hace meses.
Ellos me llaman y yo no contesto.
Mi familia dice que estoy siendo dramático.
Nadie entiende que no es que no quiera, es que no puedo.
¿Eso tiene algún sentido?
Me siento como si estuviera viendo mi vida desde afuera.
Como si nada me importara realmente.
A veces pienso que sería más fácil desaparecer.
No en serio, solo en el sentido de no tener que cargar tanto.
¿Debería hablar con un médico?

turns.prompt: What are three things you felt grateful for today?
turns.user_entry: I'm grateful for the warm cup of coffee this morning, the phone call from my daughter, and the fact that my knees didn't hurt as much today.

turns.prompt: Which of those moments felt most significant to you, and why?
turns.user_entry: The call from my daughter. She's so busy and she still makes time for me. It reminded me I'm not forgotten.

turns.prompt: Describe a worry that has been occupying your mind lately.
turns.user_entry: I keep worrying about my health. Every little pain makes me think something is seriously wrong. I know I'm probably overreacting but I can't turn off the fear.

turns.prompt: When you imagine the worst outcome, what specifically are you most afraid of?
turns.user_entry: Being a burden to my children. Getting very ill and not being able to care for myself. I don't want them to have to give up their lives for me.

turns.prompt: What has helped you manage similar fears in the past?
turns.user_entry: Prayer, honestly. And talking to my sister. She always puts things in perspective. But she's been dealing with her own issues lately so I don't want to add to her load.

turns.prompt: What might you say to a close friend who came to you with the same worry?
turns.user_entry: I'd tell her she's not alone and that her children love her. That they wouldn't see her as a burden. Maybe I should say that to myself more.

turns.prompt: What does a meaningful day look like for you now that you're retired?
turns.user_entry: I'm still figuring it out honestly. I worked for 35 years and now I don't know what to do with myself. I want to feel useful.

turns.prompt: What activities or interests from earlier in your life have you put aside that you might want to revisit?
turns.user_entry: I used to paint. I was pretty good. I stopped when the kids were young and just never went back. I still have all my supplies in the garage.

turns.prompt: What would it mean to you to pick painting back up?
turns.user_entry: It would feel like reclaiming a piece of myself. Like I still have something that's just mine.

turns.prompt: What is one small step you could take this week toward that?
turns.user_entry: Honestly, just going into the garage and opening the box. That sounds silly but it's been years and I'm a little scared to see if I still have it.

turns.prompt: Is there a chapter of your life you feel you haven't fully made peace with?
turns.user_entry: My childhood. My father was a difficult man. Not abusive in the physical sense but very cold and critical. I never felt good enough.

turns.prompt: How do you think those early experiences have shaped who you are today?
turns.user_entry: I became a perfectionist. I push myself too hard. I also find it hard to accept compliments. And I've been hard on my own children sometimes, which I regret.

turns.prompt: What would it look like to extend some compassion to your younger self?
turns.user_entry: I've never thought about that. I guess I'd tell that little boy that he was doing his best, that he was enough, even if his father couldn't see it.

turns.prompt: What feelings come up when you imagine saying those words to yourself?
turns.user_entry: Sadness. Relief maybe. A little anger. Mostly sadness. I wish someone had told me that back then.

turns.prompt: You mentioned you've sometimes been hard on your own children. Is there anything you'd like to do differently going forward?
turns.user_entry: Yes. Tell them I'm proud of them more. Not just when they achieve something. Just because they exist.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué tres cosas agradeces de hoy?
turns.user_entry: Agradezco que amanecí bien, que mi nieto me llamó para contarme sus travesuras, y que mi vecina me trajo tamales.

turns.prompt: ¿Cuál de esos momentos te tocó más el corazón y por qué?
turns.user_entry: La llamada de mi nieto. Tiene ocho años y todavía quiere contarme todo. Espero que eso no cambie.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué pensamientos te han estado generando más angustia últimamente?
turns.user_entry: Me preocupa mucho la situación económica. Con la pensión no alcanza y tengo que pedirle ayuda a mis hijos. Me da mucha vergüenza.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué sientes cuando tienes que pedir esa ayuda?
turns.user_entry: Me siento un fracaso. Toda mi vida trabajé para ser independiente y ahora dependo de ellos. Aunque ellos dicen que no les molesta, a mí sí me pesa.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué le dirías a un amigo que estuviera en la misma situación?
turns.user_entry: Que no hay vergüenza en necesitar ayuda. Que la familia existe para eso. Pero es más fácil decirlo que vivirlo.

turns.prompt: ¿Cómo describirías tu relación con tu familia en este momento de tu vida?
turns.user_entry: Complicada. Amamos pero herimos. Mis hijos ya son adultos y tenemos visiones muy distintas del mundo.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué aspecto de esa relación te gustaría mejorar?
turns.user_entry: La comunicación. A veces siento que hablamos pero no nos escuchamos. Cada quien está en su mundo.

turns.prompt: Piensa en un momento reciente en que te sentiste realmente conectado a alguien de tu familia.
turns.user_entry: El domingo pasado, comiendo todos juntos. Por un rato no hubo teléfonos y solo hablamos. Fue muy agradable. Pero duró poco.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué hiciste tú para que ese momento sucediera?
turns.user_entry: Pedí que guardaran los celulares. Fue incómodo al principio pero luego todos lo agradecieron.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué aprendiste de ti mismo en ese momento?
turns.user_entry: Que todavía puedo ser el punto de unión de la familia. Que mi presencia importa. Que no tengo que quedarme callado.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué aspectos de tu vida en este momento te generan mayor satisfacción?
turns.user_entry: Mi salud, que aunque no es perfecta, me permite hacer cosas. Y mis nietos, verlos crecer es una alegría enorme.

turns.prompt: ¿Hubo algún período difícil en tu vida del que hoy te alegras haber salido adelante?
turns.user_entry: Sí, cuando mi esposo y yo casi nos divorciamos hace 20 años. Fue muy duro pero lo superamos. Ahora somos más fuertes.

turns.prompt: ¿Qué te dice esa experiencia sobre ti mismo?
turns.user_entry: Que soy más resistente de lo que creo. Que las cosas más importantes se pueden reparar si los dos quieren.

turns.prompt: Describe in detail what you can see, hear, and feel right now in this moment.
turns.user_entry: The light coming through the window is golden. I can hear birds outside. My back aches a little but the chair is comfortable. The coffee mug in my hands is warm.

turns.prompt: When you slow down and notice these details, how does it affect how you feel?
turns.user_entry: Calmer. More grateful somehow. Like the world is actually okay even when my head says it's not.

turns.prompt: What tends to pull you out of this sense of calm during an ordinary day?
turns.user_entry: The news. Scrolling on my phone. Worrying about things I can't control. I know it doesn't help but I still do it.

turns.prompt: Is there one small thing you could change tomorrow to protect more of these moments?
turns.user_entry: Not check the news until after breakfast. Start the day in this kind of quiet. That seems doable.

turns: I don't even have words right now.
Sorry I'm being weird.
